back_translation: And the people were like complainers — evil in the ears of the LORD; and the LORD heard, and His anger was kindled, and the fire of the LORD burned among them, and consumed the edge of the camp.
And the people cried out to Moses, and Moses prayed to the LORD, and the fire died down (sank).
And he called the name of that place Taberah, because the fire of the LORD had burned among them.
And the rabble that was in their midst craved a craving; and they returned and wept, even the sons of Israel, and said: 'Who will feed us meat?'
We remember the fish which we ate in Egypt for free, the cucumbers and the melons and the leeks and the onions and the garlic.
And now our appetite (soul) is dry; there is nothing at all — nothing but the manna before our eyes.
And the manna — like the seed of coriander was it, and its appearance like the appearance of bdellium.
The people walked about and gathered it, and ground it in a handmill or pounded it in a mortar, and boiled it in a pot, and made it into cakes; and its taste was like the taste of fresh oil.
And when the dew came down upon the camp at night, the manna would come down upon it.
And Moses heard the people weeping by their families, each at the entrance of his tent; and the anger of the LORD was kindled greatly, and in Moses's eyes it was evil.
And Moses said to the LORD: 'Why have You brought evil upon Your servant? And why have I not found favor in Your eyes, that You should lay the burden of all this people upon me?'
Did I conceive all this people? If I gave birth to him, that You should say to me, 'Carry him in your bosom, as a nurse carries an infant,' to the land which You swore to their fathers?
Where shall I get meat to give to all this people? For they weep against me, saying, 'Give us meat and we will eat.'
I cannot by myself bear all this people, for it is too heavy for me.
And if You are dealing thus with me, please kill me — the killing — if I have found favor in Your eyes, and do not let me see in my misery.

And the LORD said to Moses: 'Gather to Me seventy men from the elders of Israel, whom you know to be elders of the people and their officers; and you shall take them to the Tent of Meeting, and they shall stand.'

And I will come down and speak with you there; and I will take from the Spirit that is upon you and place it upon them, and they shall bear with you in the burden of the people, and you shall not bear it by yourself.

And to the people you shall say: 'Sanctify yourselves for tomorrow, and you shall eat meat, because you have wept in the ears of the LORD, saying, "Who will feed us meat? For it was good for us in Egypt!" And the LORD will give you meat, and you shall eat.'
Not for one day shall you eat, and not for two days, and not for five days, and not for ten days, and not for twenty days,

but for a whole month of days, until it comes out from your nostrils and is loathsome to you — because you have rejected the LORD who is in your midst, and have wept before Him, saying, 'Why is this — that we came out from Egypt?'
And Moses said: 'Six hundred thousand men on foot are the people in whose midst I am, and You have said, "I will give them meat, and they shall eat for a month of days."'
Shall flocks and herds be slaughtered for them, and it would suffice for them? If all the fish of the sea were gathered for them, would it suffice for them?
And the LORD said to Moses: 'Is the hand of the LORD too short? Now you shall see whether My word will come to pass for you, or not.'
And Moses went out and spoke to the people the words of the LORD; and he gathered seventy men from the elders of the people, and had them stand around the tent.

And the LORD came down in the cloud and spoke to him; and He took from the Spirit that was upon him, and placed it upon the seventy elder men; and it happened, when the Spirit rested upon them, that they prophesied — but they did not do so again.

And two men remained in the camp — the name of one was Eldad, and the name of the second was Medad — and the Spirit rested upon them, and they were among the registered, but they had not gone out to the tent; and they prophesied in the camp.
And the young man ran and reported to Moses, and said: 'Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp.'
And Joshua son of Nun, the attendant of Moses from his youth, answered and said: 'My lord Moses, stop them!'
And Moses said to him: 'Are you jealous on my account? Who would give it that all the people of the LORD were prophets, that the LORD might place His Spirit upon them!'
And Moses was gathered (returned) to the camp, he and the elders of Israel.

And a wind set out from the LORD, and drove quail from the sea, and let them fall upon the camp, about a day's journey this way and about a day's journey that way, around the camp; and about two cubits above the surface of the earth.

And the people rose all that day and all the night and all the next day, and gathered the quail; the one who gathered least gathered ten homers; and they spread them out for themselves, a spreading, around the camp.
The meat was still between their teeth, before it was chewed off, and the anger of the LORD burned against the people, and the LORD struck the people with a very great plague.
And he called the name of that place Kibroth-hattaavah, because there they buried the people who craved.
From Kibroth-hattaavah the people set out to Hazeroth, and they remained at Hazeroth.
